pattern: A maximal run of k consecutive zeros contributes k*(k+1)/2 subarrays, so counting can be done by accumulating the current run length in one pass.
hints: Every all-zero subarray lies entirely inside one maximal block of consecutive zeros.
A block of length k yields 1 + 2 + ... + k subarrays; extend the running length each zero and reset on a nonzero.
Walk the array keeping a counter of the current zero-run length; add that counter to the total at every position (it is zero at nonzeros).

edge_inputs.description: two zero blocks split by a nonzero
all zeros maximizes the quadratic count

stress.description: long array of mostly zeros to stress the running-sum overflow path
stress.note: long zero runs produce large totals; checks 64-bit accumulation
